title: CMake: Linker flags are lost when the verilated project is not the top-level executable
body:
This issue occurs only when threading is used and you have another level of targets after the target which has the verilator output associated with it.

Example:
```cmake
add_library(veri_lib some_source.cpp)

find_package(verilator HINTS $ENV{VERILATOR_ROOT})
verilate(veri_lib top_hdl.v THREADS 2)

add_executable(self_tests self_test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(self_tests PUBLIC veri_lib)

add_executable(manual_tests manual_test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(manual_tests PUBLIC veri_lib)
```

The generator expression in [verilator-config.cmake.in:337](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/verilator-config.cmake.in#L337) won't apply anymore and both executables are missing the thread and atomic library.

I added 2 executables just to illustrate a reasonable application. You save build time this way (files in `veri_lib` are only compiled once) without having to use ccache.

I'll create a pull request which fixes this issue.
code_blocks:
add_library(veri_lib some_source.cpp)

find_package(verilator HINTS $ENV{VERILATOR_ROOT})
verilate(veri_lib top_hdl.v THREADS 2)

add_executable(self_tests self_test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(self_tests PUBLIC veri_lib)

add_executable(manual_tests manual_test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(manual_tests PUBLIC veri_lib)
